title: Sleep Network Overlay — Visual Cuelist
description: artifacts/cuelist.json — the visual overlay cuelist that drives every render / placement / drift gate (qa_card_bounds, qa_element_overlap, qa_min_hold, qa_cue_lifecycle, qa_source_collision, qa_clip_no_extra_chyron, qa_card_timing, qa_asset_coverage, qa_asset_sourcing). anchor_phrase is the Whisper timing spine; a non-dropped cue without it is a silent hole the drift gates cannot catch, so the schema requires it. additionalProperties stays open so the compiler may carry extra per-cue fields, but every known field is type-checked.

source_video.description: path to the locked source MP4 the overlay rides on

kind.description: photo_card | lower_third | chart | map | animation | clip | avatar | chapter_title | stat_card | reveal | ... (gates classify by kind/prefix)

bbox.description: pixel geometry in a 1920x1080 canvas, top-left origin. Optional — only visual cues with geometry carry it (qa_card_bounds / qa_element_overlap).

anchor_phrase.description: a contiguous word-run in the source Whisper transcript this cue fires on (the timing spine)

fallback_absolute_s.description: numeric fallback time when anchor_phrase cannot resolve (drift gates accept it instead of NOT_FOUND)

treatment.description: render treatment for the cue (e.g. hero_cutout, lower_third, chapter_title_with_plate)

frame.description: approved clip-treatment frame name (clip cues only)

grade.description: locked clip-treatment grade applied (clip / image cues)

event_cue_id.description: the visual event id an impulsive SFX binds to (qa_sfx_event_sync, from the sound cuelist)

sourcing.description: provenance for qa_asset_sourcing — proves the copyright / potential-copyright space was searched, or documents an AI generation.

_dropped.description: true if this cue was intentionally scrapped (coverage gate sees a documented drop, not a silent hole)
